_meta.description: Data dictionary for Convelo Submission Stats

hit_ratio.definition: The percentage of quoted submissions that result in a bound (sold) policy. Core KPI for measuring placement success.

loss_ratio.definition: Incurred losses divided by earned premium, expressed as a percentage. Measures how unprofitable a policy or book of business is.
loss_ratio.typical_range: For Property and Casualty Insurance the avg loss ratios fall between 40% and 60%. Below 60% is generally profitable; above 100% means losses exceed premium.

carrier_appetite.definition: A carrier's willingness to underwrite a specific risk based on industry, line of business, hazard, geography, and/or account size. Measured by historical accept/decline rates.
carrier_appetite.how_to_measure: Query quotes table grouped by carrier_id, calculate decline rate and average premium for a given line of business and division
carrier_appetite.sql_snippet: SELECT carrier_id, COUNT(*) AS total_quotes, SUM(CASE WHEN quote_status='declined'THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS declined, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN quote_status='declined' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS decline_rate FROM quotes GROUP BY carrier_id

submission.definition: A formal request sent to one or more carriers to obtain quotes for a specific account and coverage period. One submission can generate multiple quptes (one per carrier).

quote.definition: A carrier's response to a submission. One submission generates multiple quotes, one per carrier approached. A quote can be accepted, declined, or not taken up.

bind.definition: The moment a quote is formally accepted and coverage goes into effect. Only one quote per submission gets bound. Identified by quote_status = 'bound'.

ims_status.definition: Tracls where a submission is in the pipeline from intake to policy expiration.

values.in rating: Carrier is actively pricing the risk
values.referred to carrier: Broker escalated directly to carrier underwriter
values.bound-issued: Bound and policy documents formally issued
values.not taken up: Carrier quoted but client chose another
values.lost: Submission did not result in a bind, manually marked

priority.definition: A submission quality score from a 0-5 assigned by the broker indicating how complete and bindable the submission is. Higher number means a higher quality, more complete submission.

values.0: No opportunity, log in only, does not fit appetite
values.1: Received application and some loss data, account not qualified
values.2: Received application, account qualified, additional info requested
values.3: Fully qualified submission, all required information obtained, no commitment from broker
values.4: Fully complete submission, broker requirements met, recommended, broker new opportunity
values.5: Fully complete submission, broker requirements met, recommended, broker controls

account_tier.definition: Internal segmentation of accounts by revenue size.

values.Medium: Annual revenue under $250,000 but greater than $10,000

brokers.description: Internal producers (agents/brokers) who manage client relationships.

carriers.description: Insurance companies we place our clients with based off of carrier appetite.

submission_groups.description: Groups all lines of business submitted together for one client in one marketing effort or renewal cycle. One group can contain multiple submissions, one per line of business. The group tracks the total target premium for the entire account.

submissions.description: One row per line of business within a submission group. Tracks the full lifecycle of a single coverage request from intake through bind or loss. The control_numbr is the unique identifier inherited from the IMS system.

quotes.description: Individual carrier responses to a submission. Multiple quotes per submission (one per carrier approached).

policies.description: a written contract ratifying the legality of an insurance agreement.

losses.description: Claims filed against active policies. One policy can have multiple losses. Tracks payment status and amounts at the individual claim level.

example_questions_and_sql.question: What is our overall hit ratio?
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN quote_status='bound' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS hit_ratio_pct FROM quotes

example_questions_and_sql.question: What is the hit ratio by carrier?
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT carrier_id, COUNT(*) AS total_quotes, SUM(CASE WHEN quote_status='bound' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS bound_count, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN quote_status='bound' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS hit_ratio_pct FROM quotes GROUP BY carrier_id ORDER BY hit_ratio_pct DESC

example_questions_and_sql.question: Assess carrier appetite for a specific line of business
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT q.carrier_id, COUNT(*) AS total_quotes, SUM(CASE WHEN q.quote_status='declined' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS declined_count, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN q.quote_status='declined' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS decline_rate_pct FROM quotes q JOIN submissions s ON q.submission_control_number=s.control_number WHERE s.lob_name='General Liability' GROUP BY q.carrier_id ORDER BY decline_rate_pct ASC

example_questions_and_sql.question: Which accounts have the worst loss ratios?
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT a.account_name, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(l.incurred_amount) / NULLIF(SUM(p.bound_premium), 0), 2) AS loss_ratio_pct FROM losses l JOIN policies p ON l.policy_id=p.policy_id JOIN submissions s ON p.control_number=s.control_number JOIN accounts a ON s.account_id=a.account_id GROUP BY a.account_name ORDER BY loss_ratio_pct DESC LIMIT 20

example_questions_and_sql.question: How much total bound premium do we have by line of business?
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT s.lob_name, ROUND(SUM(p.bound_premium), 2) AS total_premium, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count FROM policies p JOIN submissions s ON p.control_number=s.control_number GROUP BY s.lob_name ORDER BY total_premium DESC

example_questions_and_sql.question: Which carriers have the highest decline rates?
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT carrier_id, COUNT(*) AS total_quotes, SUM(CASE WHEN quote_status='declined' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS declined_count, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN quote_status='declined' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS decline_rate_pct FROM quotes GROUP BY carrier_id ORDER BY decline_rate_pct DESC

example_questions_and_sql.question: What are the top 10 accounts by bound premium?
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT a.account_name, a.account_tier, ROUND(SUM(p.bound_premium), 2) AS total_premium FROM policies p JOIN submissions s ON p.control_number=s.control_number JOIN accounts a ON s.account_id=a.account_id GROUP BY a.account_name, a.account_tier ORDER BY total_premium DESC LIMIT 10

example_questions_and_sql.question: What is the hit ratio by division?
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT s.division, COUNT(*) AS total_quotes, SUM(CASE WHEN q.quote_status='bound' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS bound_count, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN q.quote_status='bound' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS hit_ratio_pct FROM quotes q JOIN submissions s ON q.submission_control_number=s.control_number GROUP BY s.division ORDER BY hit_ratio_pct DESC

example_questions_and_sql.question: What submissions are currently open?
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT s.control_number, a.account_name, s.lob_name, s.division, s.ims_status, s.effective_date FROM submissions s JOIN accounts a ON s.account_id=a.account_id WHERE s.ims_status IN ('submitted', 'in rating', 'underwriting review', 'referred to carrier') ORDER BY s.effective_date ASC

example_questions_and_sql.question: What is the total incurred losses by line of business?
example_questions_and_sql.sql: SELECT s.lob_name, ROUND(SUM(l.incurred_amount), 2) AS total_incurred FROM losses l JOIN policies p ON l.policy_id=p.policy_id JOIN submissions s ON p.control_number=s.control_number GROUP BY s.lob_name ORDER BY total_incurred DESC
